steps.title: Identify x-intercepts from factored form
steps.content: The function $f(x) = 3(x-1)(x-8)(x-11)$ is already in factored form. X-intercepts occur where $f(x) = 0$, which means we need the product of all factors to equal zero.

steps.title: Read the zeros from each factor
steps.content:
When $f(x) = 0$, at least one factor must equal zero. Since the constant 3 is never zero:
$$x - 1 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 1$$ $$x - 8 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 8$$ $$x - 11 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 11$$
Any of these—1, 8, or 11—is an x-coordinate of an x-intercept.
